Overall the game is really fun and unique the base is fun to try to get rare bags for and  make high star versions. Also the fighting arena against other players is really fun and  so is the NPC arena trying to get to high waves. It’s also fun trying to find the rare gold orbs and just explore hidden parts of the map. Also I don’t get why people say it has bad graphics when it doesn’t or that it’s just a tycoon game when that’s just a small fraction of the game. My only criticism is that the maximum slots for your base is 7 so it’s hard to get high star bags. But other than that the game is peak you should defiantly try it.

Punch Party is another VR game that uses the same boring, mindless Gorilla Tag movement and tries to blend it with a fighting game and fails miserably once it starts to really prioritize the AI slop riddled throughout the already bad game, the ridiculously overpriced cosmetics that look like something straight out of a Dollar Tree PS2 game, the lame gameplay that takes no skill and can easily be exploited if you team with other people or spam fly away and generally do things that have no counterplay to them and the obnoxious playerbase that is the rejects of the worst parts of Gorilla Tag. Easily missable and should easily be kept that way.

Congrats for making me break a sweat playing VR since Gorilla Tag. The grind was not worth it.

To put it simply, Punch Party is a mindless slog of a tycoon, posing as a workout/fighting game. The gameplay loop was fun at first, you grind up your strength level, and put it to the test by knocking various items into the stratus with your fists alone, but the longer you play for, the more damning it becomes that it is just trying to put you in a constant stasis of wanting progress, but never getting it.

In the game, there are a multitude of items that can be bought to be added to your gym, each having a supply in which they run out of stock for. At least, that’s what they want you to think. In reality, each restocking event (which happens every 2 1/2 minutes) randomly makes things either in or out of stock, without rhythm or reason. Curiously enough, items are less likely to be in stock the rarer they are, which if you know anything about supply and demand, makes absolutely no sense. This leaves your strength leveling stuck at a linear degree, which is not easy to wrap your head around thanks to the quantitive degree items in-game tend to follow. 

The game also seems like it’s constantly fighting for your attention. There are a plethora of trends and memes wedged into the game, with varying levels of relevancy, which will only get more dated over time. The entire PVE game mode is fought against Tralalero Tralala, for pete’s sake. It’s like jingling keys, and it drives me up the wall.

But hey, the combat’s good, isn’t it? It’s gotta be! It’s the whole point of the game- Huh? What’s that? Oh.

The combat’s not good. The gloves are jank, they clip through the floor when you form a fist, which causes some funny, yet immersion-breaking bugs, the system that determines whether a hit is hard or not is much too strict, and the very things you strike either don’t move far away enough to suggest an impact, or are knocked so far back in response that you’d have better luck catching lightning in a bottle than striking it twice.

Overall, this game is incredibly disappointing. I come in, expecting a chaotic gambit with fists swinging in all directions, with intricate maps aplenty to complement the rage, and I get game better suited for Roblox than the Meta Quest store. Unbelievable.

reviews.review_description:
First of all Punch party is a game full of fun and exercise. This game allows people to have a great time and never get bored.

They keep the game fresh by releasing new updates often. And even have an in game currency so you can buy things. Aswell as that the game has a great community with barely any toxic people.

I have seen another review from 3 months ago saying how bad the game was, but just today I got the game and it took me a bit to get control of the mechanics, but otherwise, it is very fun to play especially with my friends in a private code and I highly recommend this game.

This game really give the tycoon vibes and I love it! I also like how the game has a variety of different workout and training objects for your gym and it really makes it a fun way of grinding for strength. Also this game doesn’t have that much toxic kids in this game and overall has a very nice community. And the mechanics for fighting are very funny as-well and very simple
 To use. Overall I recommend this game for people who want to find good combat games and games that have a tycoon like vibe. PEAK

Here's a list of what I think should be added to punch party.

.Snap turn, it would be very useful for when im playing on charge

.More gamemodes. Example, one where you see who can puch something the farthest

.A land extension you can buy to be able to have more stuff in you're training area.

That's all for now, I would definitely recommend this game loads of fun with friends, have fun and stay punching

The game and the concept is so good and since it’s still in its beta I would really appreciate the creators to add more features to it to make a better experience for everyone, I would appreciate if we can report people since this should be a safe place for everyone to play so bullying and using profanity or bad language can be reported. Also purchasing new items is very expensive for one mitten shows pack for 45 dollars with tax why? That is very discouraging. Overall is very entertaining

developer_response.body: Hey! I appreciate your honest feedback and that you found it very entertaining. To address your concerns about pricing and reporting... We have gloves available for $5 if you're more comfortable with that price point. You can report folks by going to the Player Board in the Computer Room which will show you how to report through Meta's Report Abuse system which goes straight to our team. We have a moderation team formed of community members who review all reports, and we ban players for bullying and profanity.
